jira.notes: PA-OC-00 uses epic WSA-3758 (not 3267+0). PA-OC-01..11 use WSA-(3267+n) for n=1..11 => WSA-3268..WSA-3278. dependency_edges[] is canonical for PA-Outcome-map.html Dependency Flow; edit pa-outcomes.json first, then refresh embedded fallback in PA-Outcome-map.html if present.

outcomes.title: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete
Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete
Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined

dependency_edges.timing: POC band (weeks 1–2 on this timeline) must satisfy PA-OC-00 success criteria before PA-OC-01 is treated complete; foundation work may overlap late POC only where risks are documented.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Hard prerequisite: infrastructure & architecture proof (runner reachability, repo integration, at least one exercised automation path) so PA-OC-01 does not redo unresolved POC-scope unknowns.
dependency_edges.cross_cutting: PA-CW-01 (Visibility Infrastructure): Dan's security review and PA-OC-01 WP 0.3 baseline should be coordinated with VI; biweekly security sync per PA-WBS §8.

dependency_edges.timing: PA-OC-02 cannot be fully validated until PA-OC-01 exit. Per WBS parallel note: specific inventory work packages may start earlier (e.g. Ansible-based discovery) but credential validation and closure still depend on access/security baseline.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Environment access, deployment process documentation, security baseline, and network path knowledge needed before inventory is trustworthy.

decisions.text: GitHub runner topology preference (tools VPC vs per-region vs hosted).
decisions.required_by: PA-OC-01 exit (feeds later PA-D-07 Type 1 lock-in)

decisions.text: Secret management approach (GitHub Secrets, Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).
SOC 2 compliance standard for pipeline artifacts.
Data classification standard for pipeline artifacts (GRC review).
Priority ordering for config types beyond NGINX.
Credential rotation and management tooling.

granular: §9 gate: runner model, secrets, SOC 2 baseline, and data classification resolved before treating OC-01→OC-02 transition as clean.

dependency_edges.timing: Export methods, repo structure, and runner POC need an accurate NGINX config inventory and credential plan.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Inventory of NGINX configs + credential/secret management plan (no raw secrets in VCS) before migration design commits.

decisions.text: Priority ordering for config types beyond NGINX.
Credential rotation and management tooling.

granular: Complete PA-OC-02.3 before config migration (PA-R-X7 mitigation).

dependency_edges.timing: Migration execution starts after export method, repo structure, branching, and runner/Ansible feasibility are defined. PA-D-13 must be understood before PA-OC-04 entry (WBS dependency not shown on §3 mermaid alone).
dependency_edges.prerequisite: PA-OC-03 deliverables complete; plus CAB/change process understood before production config migration.

decisions.text: Repository structure pattern (by config type, client, environment).
Canonical config format per type.
GitHub runner deployment model (AWS account, VPC).
Ansible automation feasibility (no blocking MFA/human steps).
CAB change request process for pipeline deployments (lead time, evidence).

dependency_edges.cross_cutting: PA-CW-02 (Work Management): pipeline tasks should live in the same work system as operational readiness (should).
granular: §9: repo structure, format, runner infra, Ansible feasibility + PA-D-13 before productive migration.

dependency_edges.timing: Build pipeline operates on Git-backed configs; align CAB approval timing with production migration per PA-OC-04.1.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Configs in Git as source of truth; secret scan clean; branch protection/CODEOWNERS in place.

decisions.text: CAB process (already required at PA-OC-04 entry; affects production migration).
Drift alerting channel (PagerDuty vs Slack) — also cited at PA-OC-05 entry for build-adjacent ops.

dependency_edges.timing: Drift compares live state to Git; can run in parallel with PA-OC-05/06 once PA-OC-04 completes (key parallelism in WBS).
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Git is the declared source of truth before drift metrics are meaningful.

decisions.text: Drift detection alerting channel (PagerDuty vs Slack).
CAB / change discipline for production-impacting work.

dependency_edges.cross_cutting: PA-CW-03 (Learning System): drift metrics should align with operational scorecard definitions (should).

dependency_edges.timing: Deploy pipeline needs confirmed runner connectivity and Ansible automation feasibility before it can be fully validated.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Runner strategy POC and "no human-in-the-loop" validation per PA-OC-03.3.

decisions.text: Runner placement and AWS/VPC model.
Ansible feasibility; if infeasible, deploy mechanism must be redesigned (PA-R-X4).

dependency_edges.timing: Recommended sequence: deploy development can start in parallel, but full deploy validation needs build artifacts.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Should dependency — can proceed with documented risk acceptance if build lags.

dependency_edges.timing: Reliability outcome uses a 2-week rolling window (up to 3 iterations) once build is operational.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Build pipeline operational and producing repeatable artifacts.

decisions.text: Config-as-code enforcement policy — must be decided by PA-OC-08 exit so PA-OC-09 can start.

granular: §9: 90%+ success targets for build/deploy/drift before advancing.

dependency_edges.timing: Same validation window as other pipelines; deploy success rate measured over 2 weeks.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Deploy pipeline operational including rollback trust.

decisions.text: Enforcement policy alignment before leaving reliability gate.

dependency_edges.timing: Drift must meet accuracy/false-positive targets during the same validation period.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Scheduled drift job, fetch from live servers, alerting paths live.

decisions.text: Enforcement policy inputs (escalation expectations).

dependency_edges.cross_cutting: PA-CW-04 (Visibility Infrastructure): when VI SDLC ingestion is available, pipeline events should emit for CFR/DORA (should).

dependency_edges.timing: PA-OC-09 entry requires PA-OC-08 complete AND PA-D-10 approved (WBS §4 PA-OC-09 dependencies) — explicit gate missing from §3 graph alone.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: 90%+ reliability (or iteration policy exhausted), rollback trusted, dashboard operational.

decisions.text: Config-as-code enforcement policy (pipeline-only path, exceptions).
decisions.required_by: Before PA-OC-09 entry (also listed PA-OC-08 exit)

dependency_edges.cross_cutting: PA-CW-05 (Incident Response): IRP updated after PA-OC-08 and before enforcement (must per §8).
granular: Type 1: organizational commitment; no auto-default per §5.

dependency_edges.timing: Activates only if validation fails after max 3 cycles; manual processes stay available until resolved.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: Contingent: reliability below threshold after iteration policy exhausted.
granular: Fallback is planned recovery, not ad-hoc failure response.

dependency_edges.timing: Triggers after PA-OC-09 success and a leadership decision to extend the framework beyond NGINX.
dependency_edges.prerequisite: End-to-end NGINX framework proven; config landscape from PA-OC-02 informs PA-OC-11.1 prioritization.

decisions.text: Strategic commitment to replicate pipeline pattern to additional config types (one-way door for funding/roadmap).
Ordering among non-NGINX config types (feeds replication sequencing).
decisions.required_by: Informed by PA-OC-02; reaffirmed at replication

granular: Out of scope reminder: application deployment (Caley) remains separate (PA-D-14 resolved).
